x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces HRA's open reference and caseload data (programs, centers, caseload statistics, case actions), exposes the open-source ACCESS NYC eligibility rules as a screening tool, and adds apply_for_benefits — the net-new write workflow locked today inside the ACCESS HRA portal.

server.name: io.nyc.hra
server.title: NYC Human Resources Administration (HRA/DSS)
server.description: Agent-native access to the NYC Human Resources Administration / Department of Social Services — public-benefit programs (SNAP, Cash Assistance, Medicaid, HEAP, Fair Fares, HASA), the centers and offices where residents apply, aggregate caseload statistics, and case actions from NYC Open Data; plus the open-source ACCESS NYC eligibility screener and the net-new ability to apply for and track a benefits application that today lives only in the ACCESS HRA portal.
server.instructions: Use find_programs / get_program to explain benefit programs and how to apply. find_centers locates Benefits Access Centers, SNAP Centers, Medicaid Offices, and Homebase locations (with wait times where published). find_caseload_statistics and find_case_actions return AGGREGATE figures only — HRA never exposes an individual case or resident. screen_eligibility runs a household profile against the open-source ACCESS NYC rules and is read-only (nothing is filed). apply_for_benefits (net-new) submits a public-benefit application; it requires an authenticated resident and mutates state — confirm the program(s), applicant, and household with the user before submitting, and set emergency=true for immediate needs (no food, eviction, no heat) so it routes to expedited handling.

tools.name: find_programs
tools.title: Find Programs
tools.description: Search HRA/DSS benefit programs and services (SNAP, Cash Assistance, Medicaid, HEAP, Fair Fares, HASA) by category, population served, or free text.

tools.name: get_program
tools.title: Get Program
tools.description: Fetch a single benefit program (description, eligibility summary, how to apply) by its program code.

tools.name: find_centers
tools.title: Find Centers
tools.description: List HRA centers and offices — Benefits Access Centers, SNAP Centers, Medicaid Offices, and Homebase locations — by borough, center type, or ZIP, with the latest wait time where published.

tools.name: find_caseload_statistics
tools.title: Find Caseload Statistics
tools.description: Search AGGREGATE benefit caseload and enrollment figures (SNAP/Cash Assistance/Medicaid recipients, cases, enrollees) by program, measure, borough, or period. Never returns individual cases.

tools.name: find_case_actions
tools.title: Find Case Actions
tools.description: Search AGGREGATE Cash Assistance / SNAP case actions — rejections, closings, and reopenings — by action, reason code, or year, broken out by district, race, age, and sex. Counts only, never individual cases.

tools.name: screen_eligibility
tools.title: Screen Eligibility
tools.description: Screen a household against NYC benefit programs using the open-source ACCESS NYC rules and return one determination per program. Read-only — nothing is filed or stored. Use it to tell a resident what they likely qualify for before helping them apply.

tools.name: list_my_applications
tools.title: List My Applications
tools.description: List the authenticated resident's own benefits applications and their case status — today visible only inside the ACCESS HRA portal.

tools.name: get_application
tools.title: Get Application
tools.description: Fetch one of the authenticated resident's benefits applications / cases by its application ID.

tools.name: apply_for_benefits
tools.title: Apply for Benefits
tools.description: Submit an HRA public-benefit application (SNAP, Cash Assistance, Medicaid, and others). NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — it lives only in the ACCESS HRA portal or an in-person visit to a Benefits Access / SNAP Center. Requires an authenticated resident. Mutates state — confirm the program(s), applicant, and household first; set emergency=true for immediate needs (no food, eviction, no heat) to route to expedited handling.
